creator: arundel@FreeBSD.org
text:
more problems have been discovered and a mail has been sent to clusteradm@. no
response has been received yet.

the script can now be found here [1].

cheers.
alex

[1] http://people.freebsd.org/~arundel/scripts/archive-errors.sh

----- Forwarded message from Alexander Best <arundel@freebsd.org> -----

Date: Tue, 20 Jul 2010 11:58:06 +0000
From: Alexander Best <arundel@freebsd.org>
To: clusteradm@freebsd.org
Subject: problem with archived mailinglists

hi there,
 
i hacked up this script to see if there are any problems with the archived mailinglists on freefall/hub:

#!/bin/sh
cd /home/mail/archive ; for i in 19*/*/*.gz 20*/*/*.gz; do zcat $i > /dev/null 2>> /tmp/archive-errors; done

i've attached the result of that script (executed on 20th of july).

i'm not sure what caused gzip to append the trailing garbage for the 1997/8 archives. maybe the backup script contained a bug back then which was fixed in 1998? 

it's quite easy to fix these warnings since zcat will strip away the trailing garbage. so `zcat $file | gzip -9 $file` for each of those files should do the trick.

no idea what happend to '1996/freebsd-questions/19961208.freebsd-questions.gz' and if it's fixable. also i haven't got a clue what the deal with the "unexpected end of file" warnings is in connection with the ctm-announce archives. while the previous errors seem to have 
been fixed in the script and don't seem to occur anylonger, it seems the problem with the ctm-announce archives still exists atm. 

cheers.
alex
